blocks.content:
Congratulations on completing this journey! Job well done!

We hope you've enjoyed traveling with us as you:

- Learned why combining data from multiple sources matters for unified monitoring
- Created a panel using the Mixed data source with two separate queries
- Applied a Join by field transformation to combine query results on a shared time column
- Used Table view and the debug toggle to verify the combined output

---

### More to explore (optional)

The world is your oyster! Read more about how you can:

- [Transform data](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/transform-data/) with additional transformations like Merge and Concatenate
- [Share query results with another panel](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/share-query/) using the Dashboard data source
- [Write SQL expressions](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/panels-visualizations/query-transform-data/sql-expressions/) for server-side joins and complex logic

---

### Related paths

Consider taking the following paths after you complete this journey:

- [Transform data in a Grafana Cloud dashboard](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/data-transformation/)
- [Explore data using Metrics Drilldown](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/drilldown-metrics/)
